Description: Verify a user can submit a bug report with message and rating
Preconditions: User is logged in; feedback page accessible
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Feedback page 2. Select type Bug 3. Enter message 4. Give 4-star rating 5. Click Submit
Test Data: Type: bug, Message: PDF loading is slow, Rating: 4
Expected Output: Feedback submitted successfully; success confirmation shown
Actual Output: Feedback form submitted successfully; a success message was displayed and the feedback was recorded in the system

Description: Verify admin can view and approve/reject user feedback
Preconditions: Admin is logged in; at least one feedback exists
Test Steps: 1. Login as admin 2. Open Admin Dashboard 3. Navigate to Feedback section 4. Approve a feedback entry
Expected Output: Feedback status updated to Approved; visible in dashboard
Actual Output: Admin approved the feedback entry; status updated to Approved and reflected immediately in the admin dashboard

Description: Verify a user can submit a feature request through the feedback form
Preconditions: User is logged in; feedback page is accessible
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Feedback page 2. Select type Feature Request 3. Enter description 4. Give a star rating 5. Click Submit
Test Data: Type: feature, Message: Add dark mode for reading, Rating: 5
Expected Output: Feedback submitted successfully; success confirmation message shown
Actual Output: Feature request feedback submitted successfully; a success confirmation message appeared on screen and the entry was recorded in the system

Description: Verify admin can view all submitted user feedback in the dashboard
Preconditions: Admin is logged in; at least one feedback entry exists
Test Steps: 1. Login as admin 2. Navigate to Admin Dashboard 3. Open Feedback section 4. View all entries
Test Data: Admin credentials; feedback entries in database
Expected Output: All feedback entries listed with type; message; rating; status; and submitting user info
Actual Output: All feedback entries were listed in the admin panel showing type; message; star rating; status and submitting user details

Description: Verify the reading progress percentage is accurately shown in the reader
Preconditions: User is logged in; a multi-page book is open
Test Steps: 1. Open a 100-page book 2. Navigate to page 50 3. Check progress indicator
Expected Output: Progress bar or percentage shows 50% completion
Actual Output: Reading progress displayed 50% in the progress indicator reflecting correct page position

Description: Verify admin can reject a user feedback entry
Preconditions: Admin is logged in; at least one pending feedback exists
Test Steps: 1. Login as admin 2. Open Admin Dashboard 3. Navigate to Feedback section 4. Click Reject on a pending entry
Expected Output: Feedback status updated to Rejected; change reflected immediately in dashboard
Actual Output: Admin rejected the feedback entry; status was updated to Rejected and the change was immediately visible in the dashboard

Description: Verify that submitting feedback with zero star rating shows a validation error
Preconditions: User is logged in; on feedback page
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Feedback page 2. Enter a message 3. Leave rating unselected (0 stars) 4. Click Submit
Expected Output: Validation error shown; feedback not submitted until a rating is selected
Actual Output: Validation error appeared asking user to select a rating; form submission was blocked until a star rating was chosen

Description: Verify user can add a book to their bookshelf
Preconditions: User is logged in; book exists in library
Test Steps: 1. Browse books 2. Click Add to Bookshelf on a book 3. Go to Bookshelf page
Expected Output: Book appears in user's bookshelf list
Actual Output: Book was added to the bookshelf; it appeared in the Bookshelf page under the correct reading list category

Description: Verify user can remove a book from their bookshelf
Preconditions: User has at least one book in bookshelf
Test Steps: 1. Go to Bookshelf page 2. Click Remove/Trash icon on a book 3. Confirm removal
Expected Output: Book removed from bookshelf; toast notification shown
Actual Output: Book was removed from the bookshelf immediately; a toast notification confirmed the removal action

Description: Verify user can mark a bookshelf item as Currently Reading
Preconditions: User has at least one book in bookshelf with Want to Read status
Test Steps: 1. Go to Bookshelf page 2. Find a book with Want to Read status 3. Change status to Currently Reading 4. Verify update
Test Data: bookId: book in bookshelf with Want to Read status
Expected Output: Book status updated to Currently Reading; change visible in bookshelf immediately
Actual Output: Book status was updated to Currently Reading and the bookshelf reflected the change immediately

Description: Verify user can mark a bookshelf item as Completed
Preconditions: User has a book marked as Currently Reading in bookshelf
Test Steps: 1. Go to Bookshelf page 2. Find a Currently Reading book 3. Change status to Completed 4. Verify update
Test Data: bookId: book with Currently Reading status
Expected Output: Book status updated to Completed; book appears in Completed section
Actual Output: Book status changed to Completed and it appeared under the Completed section in the bookshelf

Description: Verify user can filter bookshelf books by reading status
Preconditions: User has books with different statuses in bookshelf
Test Steps: 1. Go to Bookshelf page 2. Select Completed filter 3. View filtered list
Expected Output: Only books with Completed status are shown; other statuses hidden
Actual Output: Bookshelf filtered correctly showing only books with Completed status; other statuses were hidden from view

Description: Verify that adding an already bookshelfed book shows an appropriate message
Preconditions: User has a specific book already in their bookshelf
Test Steps: 1. Browse to a book already in bookshelf 2. Click Add to Bookshelf again 3. Observe response
Test Data: bookId: book already in user's bookshelf
Expected Output: Message shown indicating book is already in bookshelf; no duplicate entry created
Actual Output: System displayed a message indicating the book is already in the bookshelf; no duplicate entry was created in the database

Description: Verify all available books are displayed on the Books page
Test Steps: 1. Login as user 2. Navigate to Books page 3. View book listings
Expected Output: Book cards with title; author; cover image displayed
Actual Output: Books page loaded all available books as cards showing title; author name; genre and cover image correctly

Description: Verify admin can add a new book with all required fields
Test Steps: 1. Login as admin 2. Open Admin Dashboard 3. Click Add Book 4. Fill in title, author, description, PDF file 5. Submit
Test Data: Title: Clean Code, Author: Robert Martin, Genre: Technology
Expected Output: New book saved; appears in book list; available to users
Actual Output: New book Clean Code was saved successfully; it appeared in the admin book list and was accessible to regular users

Description: Verify borrowing a book in BookPage.jsx
Preconditions: User is logged in. Book is available.
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to a specific BookPage. 2. Click Borrow button.
Expected Output: Button changes to Borrowed, success notification appears, and book is added to active library.
Actual Output: System updated status to Borrowed and displayed success message.

Preconditions: User is reading a borrowed book.
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Reading.jsx. 2. Turn several pages. 3. Return to Dashboard.
Expected Output: System saves the last read page. Activity Dashboard displays accurate progress percentage.
Actual Output: Reading progress successfully saved and reflected accurately on the dashboard.
